zh.lifeSummary: 當Richard Coney於1500年出生在英國英格蘭林可爾史爾波斯頓時，他的父親Sir William Kirton Lincolnshire Coney38歲，母親Alice Bell33歲。他於1519年與Elizabeth Wimberley在英國英格蘭林可爾史爾吉爾頓結婚。他們至少是2個女兒的父母。他去世於1545年4月12日在英國英格蘭林可爾史爾享年45歲，埋葬在英國英格蘭林可爾史爾巴辛索普。

nameMeanings.name: Coney
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: English: nickname from Middle English cony ‘rabbit’ (a back-formation from conies, from Old French conis, plural of conil), perhaps for someone thought to resemble a rabbit in some way or who wore rabbit skin or who sold rabbits or their skins. One York trader with this name in 1323 was a peleter or furrier.

variants.name: Cunning
meanings: Irish and Scottish (Fife): variant of Scottish or English Conning, from Middle English, Anglo-Norman French coning ‘rabbit’, used for someone thought to resemble a rabbit in some way; perhaps also an occupational name for a furrier. See also Coney .

nameMeanings.name: Richard
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: One of the most enduringly successful of the Old French personal names introduced into Britain by the Normans. It is of Germanic (Frankish) origin, derived from rīc ‘power’ + hard ‘strong, hardy’. It has enjoyed continuous popularity in England from the Conquest to the present day, influenced by the fact that it was borne by three kings of England, in particular Richard I ( 1157–99 ). He was king for only ten years ( 1189–99 ), most of which he spent in warfare abroad, taking part in the Third Crusade and costing the people of England considerable sums in taxes. Nevertheless, he achieved the status of a folk hero, and was never in England long enough to disappoint popular faith in his goodness and justice. He was also Duke of Aquitaine and Normandy and Count of Anjou, fiefs which he held at a time of maximum English expansion in France. His exploits as a leader of the Third Crusade earned him the nickname ‘Coeur de Lion’ or ‘Lionheart’ and a permanent place in popular imagination, in which he was even more firmly enshrined by Sir Walter Scott's novel Ivanhoe ( 1820 ).

variants.name: Rikert

He was king for only ten years ( 1189–99 ), most of which he spent in warfare abroad, taking part in the Third Crusade and costing the people of England considerable sums in taxes. en.lifeSummary: When Richard Coney was born in 1500, in Boston, Lincolnshire, England, United Kingdom, his father, Sir William Kirton Lincolnshire Coney, was 38 and his mother, Alice Bell, was 33. He married Elizabeth Wimberley in 1519, in Kirton, Lincolnshire, England, United Kingdom. They were the parents of at least 2 daughters. He died on 12 April 1545, in Lincolnshire, England, United Kingdom, at the age of 45, and was buried in Bassingthorpe, Lincolnshire, England, United Kingdom.

ko.Bassingthorpe with Westby, Lincolnshire,: 영국 잉글랜드 링코실르 배징소 위스 웨스트비
ko.lifeSummary: Richard Coney(이)가 1500년 영국 잉글랜드 링코실르 보스턴에서 태어났을 때, 그의 아버지 Sir William Kirton Lincolnshire Coney(은)는 38세였고, 그의 어머니 Alice Bell(은)는 33세였다. 그는 1519년 영국 잉글랜드 링코실르 커턴에서 Elizabeth Wimberley(와)과 결혼했다. 그들은 적어도 두 명의 딸을 두었다. 그는 1545년 4월 12일 영국 잉글랜드 링코실르에서 45세의 나이로 사망했으며 영국 잉글랜드 링코실르 배징소에 묻혔다.
